burial.plot_notes: Cremated; ashes at family plot, Squam Lake, New Hampshire.

notable.notable_blurb: Introduced Yoplait yogurt to the United States, 1974

parents.father_name: Augustus Witschief Bennet (US House of Representatives 1945-1947)
parents.father_research_notes: Augustus W. Bennet — US House of Representatives 1945-1947. His father William S. Bennet (US House of Representatives 1905-1911).

narrative_blocks.header: Moved to Paris — to bring Yoplait to America
narrative_blocks.body: In 1972 Bennet moved his family to Paris for a year of intensive immersion in the yogurt business, then used his Michigan dairy company to develop Yoplait USA, introducing it to the American market in 1974. He sold both companies to General Mills in 1975.

narrative_blocks.header: Amherst's most alive alumnus — "a rage to live"
narrative_blocks.body: His Amherst classmate wrote that Bennet had "a presence I can only describe as 'a rage to live'... a force that transformed each setting, venue or place." He danced, competed, built businesses and pioneered platform tennis across the Midwest — "his glass was always much more than half full."

burial.plot_notes: Cremated; ashes at family plot, Squam Lake, New Hampshire.

burial.plot_notes: Cedar Hill Cemetery Hartford CT. FindAGrave confirms. Memorial service held at Mount Auburn Cemetery Cambridge MA Nov 12 2023.

narrative_blocks.header: Every summer of her life on Squam Lake
narrative_blocks.body: From childhood to her final years, Alice spent every summer on the shores of Squam Lake in New Hampshire — mountain climber, kayaker, prankster, and spiritual guide to generations of nieces, nephews, and cousins who knew her as the heart of the family.

In 1972 she moved her family to Paris for a year, learning French cuisine and exploring art museums across three continents. On returning to Massachusetts, she became one of three founding brokers at JM Barrett in Concord, where she was the leading broker in town on multiple occasions.

sources.label: Alice Talcott Bennet obituary — Legacy.com, Oct 2023

INST018.name: Amherst College
INST018.notes: Founded 1821. One of the oldest liberal arts colleges in the United States. Merrill Edwards Gates (HD1687) served as 6th President 1890-1899. Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amherst_College David Peck Todd (HD1758) was astronomy professor and observatory director 1881-1917. Millicent Todd Bingham (HD1759) received honorary LL.D. 1957.
